title: Ensure AWS Management Console authentication failures are monitored

input_row.title: Ensure AWS Management Console authentication failures are monitored
input_row.description: Real-time monitoring of API calls can be achieved by directing CloudTrail Logs to CloudWatch Logs or an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment, and establishing corresponding metric filters and alarms. It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established for failed console authentication attempts.
input_row.rationale: CloudWatch is an AWS native service that allows you to observe and monitor resources and applications. CloudTrail logs can also be sent to an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment for monitoring and alerting. Monitoring failed console logins may decrease the lead time to detect an attempt to brute-force a credential, which may provide an indicator, such as the source IP address, that can be used in other event correlations.
input_row.audit: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following to ensure that there is at least one active multi-region CloudTrail trail with the prescribed metric filters and alarms configured: 1. Identify the log group name that is configured for use with the active multi-region CloudTrail trail: • List all CloudTrail trails: aws cloudtrail describe-trails • Identify multi-region CloudTrail trails: Trails with "IsMultiRegionTrail" set to true • Note the value associated with "Name":<trail-name> • Note the <trail-log-group-name> within the value associated with "CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn" o Example: arn:aws:logs:<region>:<account-id>:log- group:<trail-log-group-name>:* • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail is active: o aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name> § Ensure IsLogging is set to TRUE • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail captures all management events: o aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name> § Ensure there is at least one event selector for a trail with IncludeManagementEvents set to true and ReadWriteType set to All 2. Get a list of all associated metric filters for the <trail-log-group-name> captured in step 1: 3. aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name> 4. Ensure the output from the above command contains the following: 5. "filterPattern": "{ ($.eventName = ConsoleLogin) && ($.errorMessage = "Failed authentication") }" 6. Note the <console-signin-failure-metric> value associated with the filterPattern from step 3. 7. Get a list of CloudWatch alarms, and filter on the <console-signin-failure- metric> captured in step 4: 8. aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query 'MetricAlarms[?MetricName==<console-signin-failure-metric>]' 9. Note the AlarmActions value; this will provide the SNS topic ARN value. 10. Ensure there is at least one active subscriber to the SNS topic: 11. aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> • At least one subscription should have "SubscriptionArn" with a valid AWS ARN. o Example of valid "SubscriptionArn": arn:aws:sns:<region>:<account- id>:<sns-topic-name>:<subscription-id>
input_row.remediation: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following steps to set up the metric filter, alarm, SNS topic, and subscription: 1. Create a metric filter based on the provided filter pattern that checks for AWS management Console login failures and uses the <trail-log-group-name> taken from audit step 1: 2. aws logs put-metric-filter --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name> --filter-name <console-signin-failure-metric> --metric-transformations metricName=<console-signin-failure-metric>,metricNamespace='CISBenchmark',metricValue=1 --filter-pattern '{ ($.eventName = ConsoleLogin) && ($.errorMessage = "Failed authentication") }' Note: You can choose your own metricName and metricNamespace strings. Using the same metricNamespace for all Foundations Benchmark metrics will group them together. 3. Create an SNS topic that the alarm will notify: 4. aws sns create-topic --name <sns-topic-name> Note: You can execute this command once and then reuse the same topic for all monitoring alarms. Note: Capture the TopicArn that is displayed when creating the SNS topic in step 2. 5. Create an SNS subscription for the topic created in step 2: 6. aws sns subscribe --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> --protocol <sns-protocol> --notification-endpoint <sns-subscription-endpoints> Note: You can execute this command once and then reuse the same subscription for all monitoring alarms. 7. Create an alarm that is associated with the CloudWatch Logs metric filter created in step 1 and the SNS topic created in step 2: 8. aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name <console-signin-failure-alarm> --metric-name <console-signin-failure-metric> --statistic Sum --period 300 --threshold 1 --comparison-operator GreaterThanOrEqualToThreshold --evaluation-periods 1 --namespace 'CISBenchmark' --alarm-actions <sns-topic-arn>
input_row.impact: Monitoring for these failures may generate a large number of alerts, especially in larger environments.
input_row.default_value: By default, CloudTrail logs failed console authentication attempts, but no CloudWatch metric filters or alarms are configured. This means failed sign-in events are recorded, yet they are not proactively monitored or alerted on until filters and alarms are explicitly set up.
input_row.additional_information: Configuring a log metric filter and alarm on a multi-region (global) CloudTrail trail: • ensures that activities from all regions (both used and unused) are monitored • ensures that activities on all supported global services are monitored • ensures that all management events across all regions are monitored

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:** 

1. **Can AWS APIs query this property?** Yes, the control can be verified using AWS APIs, specifically the CloudTrail, CloudWatch Logs, and SNS services.
2. **Is this a standard AWS service property?** Yes, CloudTrail and CloudWatch are standard AWS services that have properties dealing with logging and monitoring.
3. **Does boto3 SDK have methods to check this?** Yes, there are methods within boto3 for CloudTrail and CloudWatch that can help verify the status of metric filters, alarms, and log groups.
4. **Does AWS CLI have commands to retrieve this?** Yes, the provided audit instructions include several AWS CLI commands to fetch the necessary information.
5. **Does it require SSH/RDP to instances?** No, it does not require SSH/RDP.
6. **Does the audit mention boto3/AWS CLI commands?** Yes, the audit section provides specified commands for verification.
7. **Is it about IAM, S3, EC2, VPC, CloudTrail, Config, KMS, etc.?** Yes, it is specifically about CloudTrail and CloudWatch, which use APIs to interact.
8. **Is it about application code, OS configs, or visual review?** No, it strictly involves AWS service configurations.

Given all criteria point towards automated capabilities, the conclusion is that this control can be audited using an automated approach.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_cloudtrail_console_authentication_failures_monitoring_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 

1. **Check Multi-Region CloudTrail:**
   - Use `boto3.client('cloudtrail').describe_trails()` or the CLI command `aws cloudtrail describe-trails` to list all trails.
   - Filter for trails with `"IsMultiRegionTrail": true`.
   - Ensure the identified trail is active by calling `boto3.client('cloudtrail').get_trail_status(Name='<trail-name>')` or using `aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name>`.
   - Confirm that `IsLogging` is set to `True`.

2. **Check Management Events:**
   - Use `boto3.client('cloudtrail').get_event_selectors(TrailName='<trail-name>')` or run `aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name>` to verify that management events are included.
   - Ensure there is at least one event selector that has `IncludeManagementEvents` set to `true` and `ReadWriteType` set to `All`.

3. **Check Metric Filters:**
   - Use `boto3.client('logs').describe_metric_filters(logGroupName='<trail-log-group-name>')` or `aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name>` to list all metric filters.
   - Validate that a filter exists with `filterPattern` matching `"{ ($.eventName = ConsoleLogin) && ($.errorMessage = "Failed authentication") }"`.

4. **Check CloudWatch Alarms:**
   - Use `boto3.client('cloudwatch').describe_alarms()` or the CLI command `aws cloudwatch describe-alarms` to list alarms, filtering on the relevant metric name from the previous step.
   - Check that there is at least one alarm triggering notifications via an SNS topic.

5. **Check SNS Subscriptions:**
   - Extract the SNS topic ARN from the alarm configuration then use `boto3.client('sns').list_subscriptions_by_topic(TopicArn='<sns-topic-arn>')` or `aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn>` to ensure at least one subscription exists.

**Pass Criteria:** 
- At least one active, multi-region CloudTrail trail.
- Management events are being logged.
- At least one metric filter exists for console login failures.
- At least one active CloudWatch alarm associated with those metrics.
- At least one subscriber exists for the SNS topic. 

This structured approach ensures thorough auditing of compliance with the specified control regarding AWS Management Console authentication failure monitoring.
